name: Vetehinen
description: The Vetehinen is a mischievous water spirit in Finnish mythology, often associated with lakes, rivers, and ponds. They are considered to be smaller and less powerful than the Ahti or Vellamo, the rulers of the waters. Vetehiset are generally malevolent, delighting in leading people astray, causing accidents, and drowning those who venture too close to the water's edge. They are also known to be playful and can sometimes be helpful, but their unpredictable nature makes them dangerous to trust. Their appearance is often described as small, ugly, and sometimes human-like, but they can also shapeshift into animals or inanimate objects to trick unsuspecting victims. It's believed they live in underwater realms, guarding hidden treasures and influencing the weather and currents. Respect for the water and caution around bodies of water are seen as important to avoid angering these spirits. They serve as a cautionary figure in Finnish folklore, reminding people of the power and unpredictable nature of water.
subtitle: Mischievous water spirit of Finland
appearance: Vetehiset are typically described as small, ugly creatures with a human-like form. Some accounts depict them as having green skin, long hair, and sharp teeth. They can also shapeshift into various forms, such as fish, logs, or even attractive humans, to lure victims into the water.
habitat: Vetehiset inhabit lakes, rivers, ponds, and other bodies of water in Finland. They are said to dwell in underwater caves and structures, guarding hidden treasures.
behavior: Vetehiset are generally malevolent and mischievous. They enjoy tricking humans, causing accidents, and drowning those who are careless around water. They can also be playful and occasionally helpful, but their unpredictable nature makes them dangerous to trust. They are easily angered by disrespect towards the water or its creatures.

keyMyths.title: The Drowning of the Careless Fisherman
keyMyths.description: A fisherman, disrespectful of the lake, mocked the Vetehinen and was lured into the water by illusions, ultimately drowning in the depths. This story highlights the dangers of disrespecting the water spirits and the consequences of carelessness.

sources.author: Martti Haavio
sources.description: A comprehensive overview of Finnish mythology.
sources.text: A comprehensive overview of Finnish mythology.

sources.author: Elias Lönnrot
sources.description: While not explicitly mentioning Vetehinen in detail, it provides context for Finnish water spirits and beliefs.
